title: What's new
headings: From intelligent coding assistants to orchestrated AI across the entire software lifecycle
text:
GitLab monthly releases

See additions and changes to the GitLab product. New releases monthly.

Plans subject to change

This page outlines the expected most important planned features of upcoming releases. We often move things around, do things that are not listed, and cancel things that are listed. This is an estimate of planned features, but is not definitive.

From intelligent coding assistants to orchestrated AI across the entire software lifecycle

BYOK Iteration 1: Token authentication with GitLab AI Gateway

BYOK Iteration 1: Token authentication with GitLab AI Gateway

Enterprises want to use their own model subscriptions to power Duo Agent Platform features, for cost control, data residency, or existing contract leverage. BYOK Iteration 1 connects customer-owned providers through token-based authentication via GitLab AI Gateway. Weâre working to add:

Connections to existing provider relationships through the AI Gateway using token authentication.

Connections to existing provider relationships through the AI Gateway using token authentication.

Support for providers exposed through the AI Gateway.

Support for providers exposed through the AI Gateway.

Preservation of existing contract leverage while gaining GitLab's agentic workflow layer.

Preservation of existing contract leverage while gaining GitLab's agentic workflow layer.

More features to Self-Hosted Duo Agent Platform and model choice introduced in earlier releases.

More features to Self-Hosted Duo Agent Platform and model choice introduced in earlier releases.

AI-powered false positive detection for secret scanning

AI-powered false positive detection for secret scanning

Security teams spend significant time triaging secret detection findings that turn out to be test credentials or dummy values. AI-powered false positive detection analyzes findings before developers see them, identifying test credentials, example values, and placeholder secrets with clear explanations and confidence scores. This detection is separate from SAST false positive detection. This capability is being built to:

Proactively identify false positives before they reach developer workflows

Proactively identify false positives before they reach developer workflows

Provide clear explanations and confidence scoring for each determination

Provide clear explanations and confidence scoring for each determination

Support bulk dismiss for validated false positives

Support bulk dismiss for validated false positives

Track precision and recall metrics to continuously improve detection accuracy

Track precision and recall metrics to continuously improve detection accuracy

Web and upload in GitLab Duo Chat (Agentic)

Web and upload in GitLab Duo Chat (Agentic)

GitLab Duo Chat (Agentic) will support file uploads and web content as first-class context, so agents can reason over documentation, logs, specs, and other artifacts alongside repository data. This expansion is intended to:

Move DAP from repo-only reasoning to cross-source troubleshooting, planning, and analysis.

Move DAP from repo-only reasoning to cross-source troubleshooting, planning, and analysis.

Let teams share logs, specs, and documentation directly into agent conversations.

Let teams share logs, specs, and documentation directly into agent conversations.

Support richer context for agentic workflows like root-cause analysis and incident triage.

Support richer context for agentic workflows like root-cause analysis and incident triage.

Reduce context-switching between external documents and GitLab.

Reduce context-switching between external documents and GitLab.

Agentic bulk vulnerability resolution

Agentic bulk vulnerability resolution

When the same vulnerability pattern appears across a codebase, creating individual merge requests for each instance creates review fatigue and slows remediation. Building on the per-vulnerability SAST resolution flow, this extension will intelligently group related findings by shared root causes and generate consolidated merge requests. Weâre planning to include:

Pattern recognition to identify similar vulnerabilities across codebases.

Pattern recognition to identify similar vulnerabilities across codebases.

Intelligent grouping of findings by root cause or remediation strategy.

Intelligent grouping of findings by root cause or remediation strategy.

Single consolidated MRs that address multiple related findings.

Single consolidated MRs that address multiple related findings.

Visual clustering in the merge request interface for clear review context.

Visual clustering in the merge request interface for clear review context.

Unified DevOps & Security

From bolt-on security and fragmented toolchains to integrated, context-aware DevSecOps

Vulnerability management across contexts (non-default branch tracking)

Vulnerability management across contexts (non-default branch tracking)

Today, GitLab only tracks vulnerabilities on the default branch, meaning organizations releasing from long-lived branches have zero visibility into security posture for code running in production. Non-default branch tracking is a top Security Insights request and a documented adoption blocker. Hereâs what we are working on:

Configuration of which branches are tracked for vulnerability management beyond the default branch.

Configuration of which branches are tracked for vulnerability management beyond the default branch.

Local scoping of vulnerability state changes to each branch with an option for global changes.

Local scoping of vulnerability state changes to each branch with an option for global changes.

Branch-aware filtering on the vulnerability report for release-specific security posture views.

Branch-aware filtering on the vulnerability report for release-specific security posture views.

Vulnerability management activity on tracked non-default branches as on default.

Vulnerability management activity on tracked non-default branches as on default.

Security dashboard attribute filters

Security dashboard attribute filters

The business-context security attributes introduced earlier need to be filterable on the Security Dashboard itself. Security managers will be able to filter dashboards by business impact, application, business unit, and more. We are planning to:

Add security attribute filters on the Security Dashboard building on context filtering.

Add security attribute filters on the Security Dashboard building on context filtering.

Integrate with existing Report Type and Project filters for combined views.

Integrate with existing Report Type and Project filters for combined views.

Let compliance teams pull scoped reports and engineering leads focus on their domain.

Let compliance teams pull scoped reports and engineering leads focus on their domain.

Add a foundation for vulnerability report attribute filtering in subsequent iterations.

Add a foundation for vulnerability report attribute filtering in subsequent iterations.

Job inputs for manual pipeline jobs

Job inputs for manual pipeline jobs

CI/CD inputs exist today for pipeline-level configuration, but developers still cannot set parameters for individual manual jobs within pipelines, forcing expensive full pipeline re-runs for simple parameter changes. Job-level inputs will bring dynamic parameter setting to manual jobs when parameters change during execution. This capability is being built to:

Allow individual job parameter configuration without full pipeline re-runs.

Allow individual job parameter configuration without full pipeline re-runs.

Support dynamic inputs based on earlier job outputs or external conditions.

Support dynamic inputs based on earlier job outputs or external conditions.

Reduce deployment errors from parameter mismatches.

Reduce deployment errors from parameter mismatches.

Provide an easy migration from other tools without needing Jenkins for enterprise CI/CD consolidation.

Provide an easy migration from other tools without needing Jenkins for enterprise CI/CD consolidation.

Security Dashboard and SBOM across contexts

Security Dashboard and SBOM across contexts

Extending non-default branch tracking to the Security Dashboard and SBOM means vulnerability trends, dependency lists, and SBOM exports all become branch-aware. This extension is intended for:

for enterprise CI/CD consolidation.

Extending non-default branch tracking to the Security Dashboard and SBOM means vulnerability trends, dependency lists, and SBOM exports all become branch-aware. This extension is intended for:

Making vulnerability trends and SBOM exports branch-aware for organizations with long-lived release branches.

Making vulnerability trends and SBOM exports branch-aware for organizations with long-lived release branches.

Supporting CycloneDX, JSON, and SPDX export formats scoped to specific branches.

Supporting CycloneDX, JSON, and SPDX export formats scoped to specific branches.

Tracking local vs. global vulnerability state changes with branch-scoped detection timestamps.

Tracking local vs. global vulnerability state changes with branch-scoped detection timestamps.

KEV/EPSS filter in MR approval policies

KEV/EPSS filter in MR approval policies

CVSS severity alone does not tell you whether a vulnerability is actively exploited or likely to be. Adding Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) and Exploit Prediction Scoring System (EPSS) as filter criteria in MR approval policies shifts enforcement from theoretical severity to real-world exploitability. This filter is being created to:

Add KEV and EPSS as filter options in MR approval policies.

Add KEV and EPSS as filter options in MR approval policies.

Support policies like "block merge if any dependency has a known exploit.

Support policies like "block merge if any dependency has a known exploit.

Focus on enforcement of findings that pose actual risk rather than theoretical severity.

One of GitLab's most requested features and a documented blocker for organizations migrating. Epics are currently group-level only, forcing teams to create unnecessary subgroups or misuse milestones. Project-level Epics are being enhanced to:

Make Epics available at the project level for Premium tier.

Make Epics available at the project level for Premium tier.

Support roadmap, board, and view availability within project context.

Support roadmap, board, and view availability within project context.

Remove the need for subgroup workarounds to group issues within a project.

Remove the need for subgroup workarounds to group issues within a project.

Address the needs of customers wanting to migrate, by providing project-scoped planning.

Address the needs of customers wanting to migrate, by providing project-scoped planning.

Native CODEOWNERS reviewer auto-assignment

Native CODEOWNERS reviewer auto-assignment

GitLab wants to bring automated CODEOWNERS assignments to address manual steps for each MR by reducing bottlenecks for developers and lowering risk with review selection. This feature is being made to:

Automatically assign reviewers based on CODEOWNERS patterns matched against MR changed files.

Automatically assign reviewers based on CODEOWNERS patterns matched against MR changed files.

Support both group and individual user assignments.

Support both group and individual user assignments.

Preserve manual override with audit trail for automatic vs. manual assignments.

Preserve manual override with audit trail for automatic vs. manual assignments.

Lay the foundation for DAP-powered intelligent reviewer selection that considers expertise and availability.

Lay the foundation for DAP-powered intelligent reviewer selection that considers expertise and availability.

Many teams hesitate to adopt work item features (custom statuses, custom fields, work item types) that are only available through GraphQL. The REST API removes this adoption blocker for integrations, automation scripts, and tools that rely on REST. This API is being created to:

Wrap existing work item Finders and Services with GraphQL-exposed operations.

Wiki contextual discussions

Wiki contextual discussions

Wiki discussions happen away from relevant content, reducing effectiveness and increasing context switching. Inline contextual commenting will tie threaded discussions directly to highlighted text, keeping conversations connected to the content they reference. We're working on:

Inline commenting on highlighted wiki content.

Inline commenting on highlighted wiki content.

Threaded discussions tied to specific text selections.

Threaded discussions tied to specific text selections.

Discussion visibility indicators on wiki pages.

Maven Virtual Registry GA and npm Virtual Registry

Maven Virtual Registry GA and npm Virtual Registry

Maven graduates to GA while npm enters beta, covering the two highest-volume enterprise artifact ecosystems. Docker Virtual Registry also targets GA in this timeframe. By mid-year, GitLab will have virtual registries at GA for Maven, Docker, and npm. These registries are being made to:

Bring Maven to production readiness with allow/deny filtering, lifecycle policies, Geo support, and analytics.

Bring Maven to production readiness with allow/deny filtering, lifecycle policies, Geo support, and analytics.

Extend the virtual registry architecture to npm for JavaScript/Node.js teams, targeting GA after beta.

Extend the virtual registry architecture to npm for JavaScript/Node.js teams, targeting GA after beta.

Move Docker Virtual Registry from experiment to GA, completing the container image solution.

Move Docker Virtual Registry from experiment to GA, completing the container image solution.

DORA metrics (deployment frequency, lead time for changes, change failure rate, time to restore service) are the industry standard for measuring software delivery performance. Complete maturity for the API means full feature parity for programmatic consumption. This milestone is meant to include:

API coverage for all four DORA metrics.

API coverage for all four DORA metrics.

Support for dashboards, executive reporting, and automated alerting without relying on the GitLab UI.

Support for dashboards, executive reporting, and automated alerting without relying on the GitLab UI.

Enhanced CI/CD job metrics and analytics improvements from earlier releases.

Enhanced CI/CD job metrics and analytics improvements from earlier releases.

Security attribute filtering on vulnerability reports

Security attribute filtering on vulnerability reports

The security attributes story that started on the Security Dashboard reaches the vulnerability report. Teams will be able to filter vulnerabilities by application, business unit, or team directly on the report, bookmark filtered URLs, and share filtered views. This filter is being developed for:

Adding business context filtering on the group-level vulnerability report.

Auto-remediation with automatic dependency bumping

Enablement-only SAST profile

Enablement-only SAST profile

SAST remains foundational to application security, but CI/CD configuration requirements create adoption barriers. The SAST scan profile will let security managers deploy static analysis across projects through Security Inventory, with automatic language detection and Advanced SAST for supported languages on Ultimate. This profile is designed to:

Auto-detect project languages and appropriate analyzers.

Auto-detect project languages and appropriate analyzers.

Automatically apply Advanced SAST for supported languages.

Automatically apply Advanced SAST for supported languages.

Support group-level management through Security Configuration and Inventory.

Support group-level management through Security Configuration and Inventory.

Follow the same interaction patterns established by secret push protection profiles.

Follow the same interaction patterns established by secret push protection profiles.

Enablement-only secret push protection profile

Enablement-only secret push protection profile

Security managers need to deploy secret push protection at scale without manual project-by-project configuration. Scan configuration profiles will introduce bulk enablement through Security Inventory, protecting projects without requiring YAML changes or developer intervention. This profile is being created to:

Provide bulk protection across projects through the Security Inventory interface.

Provide bulk protection across projects through the Security Inventory interface.

Have a zero-YAML, profile-based approach that is consistent with other scan profiles.

Have a zero-YAML, profile-based approach that is consistent with other scan profiles.

Provide project-level control for teams experiencing edge cases.

Enablement-only secret detection pipeline profile

Enablement-only secret detection pipeline profile

While secret push protection blocks commits in real-time, pipeline scanning provides complementary coverage by detecting secrets in repository history and pipeline artifacts. This profile will let security managers apply broad secret detection using the same profile-based approach, creating defense-in-depth without configuration overhead. We are building this profile to:

Scan historical repositories for previously committed secrets.

Scan historical repositories for previously committed secrets.

Cover pipeline artifacts for exposed credentials.

Cover pipeline artifacts for exposed credentials.

Provide complementary coverage alongside secret push protection.

Provide complementary coverage alongside secret push protection.

Support staggered rollout to help prevent infrastructure overload.

Support staggered rollout to help prevent infrastructure overload.

Enablement-only dependency scanning profile

Enablement-only dependency scanning profile

Completing the scan configuration profiles family, the dependency scanning profile will let security teams apply dependency scanning across projects without requiring developers to edit CI configs. We are working to create this profile for:

Dependency scanning across projects through Security Inventory.

Custom rules for secret detection

Custom rules for secret detection

Organizations with proprietary credential formats need the ability to define their own secret patterns beyond GitLab's built-in rules. Custom rules will let security teams add organization-specific patterns that are automatically enforced during secret detection. This capability is being designed to:

Allow organizations to define custom secret patterns for proprietary credential formats.

Allow organizations to define custom secret patterns for proprietary credential formats.

Enforce custom rules alongside built-in detection rules automatically.

Enforce custom rules alongside built-in detection rules automatically.

Catch organization-specific leaks that generic pattern-based rules miss.

Automated vulnerability severity customization policy

Automated vulnerability severity customization policy

Default vulnerability severities don't always reflect organizational risk priorities. Security teams will be able to automate severity overrides based on rules including KEV/EPSS, CVSS, reachability, CVE, CWE, and scan type, mapping risk to business needs while automating triage processes that previously required manual intervention. This policy is meant to:

Automate severity adjustments using KEV, EPSS, CVSS, and reachability signals.

Automate severity adjustments using KEV, EPSS, CVSS, and reachability signals.

Quickly process new vulnerabilities matching policy conditions.

Quickly process new vulnerabilities matching policy conditions.

Reduce critical/high vulnerability noise through intelligent prioritization.

Reduce critical/high vulnerability noise through intelligent prioritization.

Provide an audit trail of policy-driven severity changes for compliance visibility.

Security Manager role

Security Manager role

Security teams currently need Developer or Maintainer access to use vulnerability management features, granting far more permissions than necessary. The Security Manager role inherits from Reporter and adds security-specific permissions. This role is being made to help:

Provide vulnerability management, dashboards, policies, and compliance feature access without over-privileging.

Provide vulnerability management, dashboards, policies, and compliance feature access without over-privileging.

Introduce a non-hierarchical role that breaks the linear Guest-to-Owner inheritance model.

Introduce a non-hierarchical role that breaks the linear Guest-to-Owner inheritance model.

Support promotion on the invite members page for easy assignment.

Support promotion on the invite members page for easy assignment.

License scanning for Dart/Flutter projects

License scanning for Dart/Flutter projects

Dart and Flutter license information currently displays as UNKNOWN, forcing teams to use external tools for license compliance. License scanning support for the pub package manager fills this gap for the growing Flutter ecosystem. Weâre working on:

Creating an ingestion of license data for the pub package manager.

Generic password detection with contextual indicators

Generic password detection with contextual indicators

Pattern-based secret detection misses generic passwords like admin_password=Password123! that lack recognizable formats. Context-aware detection identifies generic passwords by analyzing surrounding code patterns and variable naming conventions, extending current secret detection coverage. Currently in the pipeline is:

Context-aware filtering to reduce false negatives for generic passwords.

Malicious package detection in dependency scanning

Malicious package detection in dependency scanning

Supply chain attacks through malicious packages pose immediate risk: unlike CVEs that may exist dormant for months, a malicious package causes harm the moment it is ingested. GitLab is exploring surfacing malicious packages directly in dependency scanning results to help teams identify and respond to active threats. We're exploring:

Critical severity flagging for malicious packages with clear "MAL-" identifiers for immediate recognition.

Critical severity flagging for malicious packages with clear "MAL-" identifiers for immediate recognition.

Detection of credential theft, data exfiltration, botnet, and database corruption attack vectors.

Integration with vulnerability reports and dependency lists for unified security visibility.

Incremental scanning for Advanced SAST

Incremental scanning for Advanced SAST

Advanced SAST analysis in large repositories can exceed pipeline job time limits. Incremental scanning will cache results from previous scans and only analyze changed code, significantly reducing scan time while producing accurate results. This is a separate performance initiative from Advanced SAST's backwards slicing work. This optimization is meant to:

Cache results from computationally expensive analysis tasks.

Cache results from computationally expensive analysis tasks.

Reduce scan time for repositories with incremental changes.

Reduce scan time for repositories with incremental changes.

Produce accurate results equivalent to non-incremental scans.

Produce accurate results equivalent to non-incremental scans.

PDF export for security dashboard

PDF export for security dashboard

Security dashboards need exportable formats for stakeholder communication and compliance documentation. PDF export will replicate dashboard panels with applied filters and group-by configurations for offline sharing and archival. This export is being created to:

SLSA verification and container image signing

SLSA verification and container image signing

Organizations need to prove the provenance and integrity of their artifacts across the supply chain. Attestation APIs, UI integration, and GitLab CLI (glab) commands for verification will validate artifacts, while container image signing and verification extends this to the container ecosystem. We are working to:

Provide attestations API for consumer lookup and verification.

Provide attestations API for consumer lookup and verification.

Integrate UI linking attestations to builds with project-level views.

Integrate UI linking attestations to builds with project-level views.

Support container image signing and verification for supply chain integrity.

Support container image signing and verification for supply chain integrity.

Offer GitLab CLI (glab) attestation verification for command-line workflows.

ons for Personal Access Tokens

Granular permissions for Personal Access Tokens

Broad PAT scopes like read_api and api create excessive blast radius if tokens are compromised. Fine-grained permissions will scope tokens to specific resources and operations, restricted to specific projects and groups. We are working on:

Replacing broad scope categories with resource-specific permissions.

Replacing broad scope categories with resource-specific permissions.

Restricting token boundaries to specific projects and groups.

Restricting token boundaries to specific projects and groups.

Enforcement across REST and GraphQL APIs with documented permission requirements.

Enforcement across REST and GraphQL APIs with documented permission requirements.

Providing credential inventory visibility for applied permissions.

Providing credential inventory visibility for applied permissions.

GitLab Secrets Manager

GitLab Secrets Manager

Teams using CI variables for secrets lack proper lifecycle management, rotation reminders, and granular access control. GitLab Secrets Manager will provide purpose-built secrets storage with expiration management, environment/branch scoping, and role-based permissions. This manager is being expanded to:

Support secret creation with expiration and environment/branch scoping.

Support secret creation with expiration and environment/branch scoping.

Provide rotation reminders for proactive credential management.

Provide rotation reminders for proactive credential management.

Security policy integration with security attributes

Security policy integration with security attributes

The security attributes introduced for tagging and filtering will extend into the policy engine. Security teams will be able to write policies that reference custom security attributes for fine-grained governance rules. Hereâs what we are working on:

Connecting security policies to business-context attributes like business impact, application, and business unit.

Scheduled Pipeline Execution Policies

Security teams will be able to enforce scheduled scans (nightly DAST, weekly dependency scanning) via policy, ensuring continuous compliance without developer action. This policy is intended to:

Enforce scheduled security scans via policy without requiring developer pipeline configuration.
